title: Expressing support for the goals and ideals of "National Hypertension Awareness Month".
plain_summary: This bill shows support for National Hypertension Awareness Month, which happens in May. High blood pressure is a serious health problem that affects almost half of all adults in America, but many people don't know they have it. The disease costs billions of dollars in medical care each year. The House of Representatives wants to keep teaching people how to prevent and treat high blood pressure, and they believe everyone should be able to get affordable healthcare to help with this condition.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.

sections.title: Resolution Title
sections.text: This resolution expresses support for the goals and ideas of National Hypertension Awareness Month.

sections.title: Background Information
sections.text: Hypertension means high blood pressure. It affects almost half of all adults in the United States and is a major cause of heart and blood vessel diseases. High blood pressure often goes unnoticed and untreated. This leads to serious health problems like heart disease, stroke, and kidney failure. It causes nearly five hundred thousand deaths each year in the United States. Only about one in four adults in the United States has their high blood pressure under control. Health care costs in the United States related to high blood pressure cost about one hundred thirty one billion dollars per year. People with high blood pressure spend nearly two thousand dollars more each year on health care than people without it. High blood pressure affects some groups more than others, including communities of color, people with less money, and those who have trouble getting health care. This creates unfair differences in who gets sick and how well they are treated. Finding high blood pressure early, managing it properly, and having access to medical treatments are very important for reducing how much it affects people's health. Medical technology and other advances continue to create new treatments that can lower blood pressure and improve heart health. The month of May is set aside to teach people about high blood pressure, help them learn how to prevent and manage it, and encourage them to check their blood pressure regularly. Having access to affordable and good quality health care services, including medical treatments, is very important for managing high blood pressure and improving health. Community programs, health care workers, public health agencies, and advocacy groups are very important in teaching the public, providing resources, and supporting policies that increase awareness about high blood pressure and access to medical care.

sections.title: What the House of Representatives Supports
sections.text: The House of Representatives supports calling May Hypertension Awareness Month. It recognizes how important it is to teach people about the risks of high blood pressure and how to prevent and manage it. The House encourages health care providers, public health agencies, and community organizations to keep working to teach and help people monitor and manage their blood pressure well. It emphasizes how important it is to have access to affordable and complete health care services, including medical treatments, to deal with high blood pressure and reduce how much it affects people and communities. The House supports programs that aim to improve health care access, make it more affordable, and teach people about managing and treating high blood pressure. It also shows appreciation to health care professionals, researchers, advocates, and organizations that work to reduce the burden of high blood pressure and improve public health.
